commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary: The PR adds JSON-backed persistence for the Slack thread participation cache, expands `sent-thread-cache` tests, and adds an API-barrel comment keeping only production cache functions exported.

target.title: fix(slack): persist thread participation cache to survive gateway restarts [claude, human developer oversight]
target.author: zeroaltitude

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: zeroaltitude
commands.command: — addressed three of your four points in commit 9c187156a5. quick status: **fixed in this commit:** 1. ✅ **test-only helpers no longer exposed through the slack api barrel.** `extensions/slack/api.ts` was doing `export *` which re-exported `_flushpersist` and `_resetfortests` as part of the public api surface. replaced with an explicit list of just the three production functions (`recordslackthreadparticipation`, `hasslackthreadparticipation`, `clearslackthreadparticipationcache`). same-process consumers can no longer redirect the persist path or trigger arbitrary writes via the public api. 2. ✅ **recency drift fixed.** `recordslackthreadparticipation` now does `delete-then-set` rather than plain `map.set` on existing keys, so map insertion order reflects actual recency. verified by a new test that records a → b → a-again and asserts the on-disk insertion order is `[b, a]`. 3. ✅ **in-memory growth bounded on insert.** the max_entries cap is now applied at insertion time (not just at serialization), so busy workspaces accumulating many unique threads between debounced persists can't balloon the in-memory map. verified by a new test that records 5005 unique entries and asserts the persisted file has exactly 5000 with the oldest 5 evicted. tests: 17/17 pass (was 15; +2 new). **not addressed in this commit, deferred to maintainer guidance:** 4. ⏳ **migration to `src/plugin-sdk/persistent-dedupe.ts`** (or a future `persistent-keyed-store` from #70626). i looked at this and the sdk helper would give us file locking, atomic writes via `writejsonfileatomically`, bounded/quarantined parsing via `readjsonfilewithfallback`, and the shared storage contract — exactly what you flagged. the blocker is that the sdk helper's api (`checkandrecord` / `hasrecent`) is async while the current public sync api is consumed in synchronous slack code paths (`prepare.ts`, `dispatch.ts`, `action-runtime.ts`). migrating would require either making the public api async (breaking change for callers) or wrapping with sync semantics (defeats the locking benefit). could a maintainer weigh in on: - is the right move to make the public api async and update the three call sites? - or should we wait for #70626 to land and adopt that interface? - or coordinate with #58731 to converge on one implementation path? i'd be happy to do the async refactor if that's the agreed direction — wanted maintainer signal first rather than churn the pr through a possibly-wrong refactor.
commands.freeform_prompt: — addressed three of your four points in commit 9c187156a5. Quick status: **Fixed in this commit:** 1. ✅ **Test-only helpers no longer exposed through the Slack API barrel.** `extensions/slack/api.ts` was doing `export *` which re-exported `_flushPersist` and `_resetForTests` as part of the public API surface. Replaced with an explicit list of just the three production functions (`recordSlackThreadParticipation`, `hasSlackThreadParticipation`, `clearSlackThreadParticipationCache`). Same-process consumers can no longer redirect the persist path or trigger arbitrary writes via the public API. 2. ✅ **Recency drift fixed.** `recordSlackThreadParticipation` now does `delete-then-set` rather than plain `Map.set` on existing keys, so Map insertion order reflects actual recency. Verified by a new test that records A → B → A-again and asserts the on-disk insertion order is `[B, A]`. 3. ✅ **In-memory growth bounded on insert.** The MAX_ENTRIES cap is now applied at insertion time (not just at serialization), so busy workspaces accumulating many unique threads between debounced persists can't balloon the in-memory Map. Verified by a new test that records 5005 unique entries and asserts the persisted file has exactly 5000 with the oldest 5 evicted. Tests: 17/17 pass (was 15; +2 new). **Not addressed in this commit, deferred to maintainer guidance:** 4. ⏳ **Migration to `src/plugin-sdk/persistent-dedupe.ts`** (or a future `persistent-keyed-store` from #70626). I looked at this and the SDK helper would give us file locking, atomic writes via `writeJsonFileAtomically`, bounded/quarantined parsing via `readJsonFileWithFallback`, and the shared storage contract — exactly what you flagged. The blocker is that the SDK helper's API (`checkAndRecord` / `hasRecent`) is async while the current public sync API is consumed in synchronous Slack code paths (`prepare.ts`, `dispatch.ts`, `action-runtime.ts`). Migrating would require either making the public API async (breaking change for callers) or wrapping with sync semantics (defeats the locking benefit). Could a maintainer weigh in on: - Is the right move to make the public API async and update the three call sites? - Or should we wait for #70626 to land and adopt that interface? - Or coordinate with #58731 to converge on one implementation path? I'd be happy to do the async refactor if that's the agreed direction — wanted maintainer signal first rather than churn the PR through a possibly-wrong refactor.
